description:
You are a performance-engineer who just ran two profiling sessions of an application.  
The raw JSON reports already exist on disk and you must now:

1. Collect the essential usage numbers from all existing run-files with **jq** and write them into a single summary file.  
2. Confirm that the resulting summary obeys a provided Draft-07 JSON-Schema and leave a very small log of the result.

Required details and constraints:

A. Source material  
   • Two files are already present in  
     /home/user/projects/profile/  
       - run1.json  
       - run2.json  
     Each file is a valid JSON array of objects.  
   • A schema file is already present in  
     /home/user/projects/profile/schema/usage_summary.schema.json

B. Build the summary  
   • Create a new file EXACTLY at:  
     /home/user/projects/profile/usage_summary.json  
   • The file must be UTF-8, pretty-printed (indented) JSON.  
   • The JSON value must be an array containing ONLY the three keys below and in this exact order for every object:  
       1. process   (string)  
       2. cpu_usage (number)  
       3. mem_usage (number)  
     No extra keys are permitted in any object.  
   • The final expected structure therefore looks like (spacing does not matter, but key order **does**):  
     [  
       { "process": "nginx",  "cpu_usage": 12.5, "mem_usage": 150.2 },  
       { "process": "redis",  "cpu_usage": 7.9,  "mem_usage": 85.0  },  
       { "process": "postgres","cpu_usage": 18.2,"mem_usage": 230.4 }  
     ]

C. Validate the summary  
   • Use a command-line JSON-Schema validator or a jq expression of your choice to check usage_summary.json against the schema file mentioned in A.  
   • Write the outcome to a **plain-text, one-line** log file located at:  
     /home/user/projects/profile/validation.log  
   • The file content must be EXACTLY one of the following two possibilities (plus a trailing newline):  
       VALID  
       INVALID

D. Success criteria checked by the grader  
   1. /home/user/projects/profile/usage_summary.json exists and is valid JSON.  
   2. Every object in that array has precisely the three required keys, in the specified order, with non-negative numeric values for cpu_usage and mem_usage.  
   3. The array contains exactly three objects (one per original record).  
   4. /home/user/projects/profile/validation.log exists and contains either “VALID\n” or “INVALID\n”.  
   5. If the schema check passes, the log must read “VALID”. If it fails, “INVALID”.

Accomplish the task using as few terminal commands as you reasonably can (two or three are enough). Do **NOT** hard-code paths inside the summary; derive every value directly from the supplied run*.json files with jq.
name: task_000000_08e2ac79
